name: Posole
recipeIngredient: 2 lb Pork roast, cut into chunks
2 each Pork shanks or pigs feet
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the meat and pork rinds along with the pork shanks in a large kettle. Add about 5 quarts of water (or enough to cover the meat). Stir in approximately 1 tablespoon salt and bring to a boil. Cook over medium heat for about 1½ hours, then remove excess grease and set aside. Reserve the liquid.
2. Wash the posole carefully until the water runs clear to remove any lime from the kernels. Return it to the kettle and cover with fresh water. Bring to a boil until the posole has popped.
3. Mix the cooked meat, posole, pork rinds, and shanks or pigs feet in the kettle. Add oregano, chopped onion, and chili pods. Simmer for about ½ hour.
4. Serve as a main dish with hard rolls, tortillas, or crackers. Additional red chili sauce may be added at serving time for extra spice.
NOTE: Posole can be found in the meat section if available; if not, hominy can be substituted in equal amounts without rinsing or pre-cooking. Omitting pork rinds, pork shanks, or pigs feet will reduce authenticity but not taste. Adding one can of tomatoes enhances flavor.
